evals.prompt: Help me organize our project management SaaS product into capability blocks. We have features spread across: task creation/management, project templates, team permissions, reporting dashboards, integrations (Slack, Jira, GitHub), time tracking, and billing. Create a block structure.
evals.expected_output: A structured product block portfolio organized by customer value, not technical architecture
expectations: Output groups features into customer-value-oriented blocks (not tech-oriented)
Output creates a block portfolio table with owner, maturity, and priority dimensions
Output assigns the listed features to appropriate blocks
Output explains the rationale for the block structure
Output identifies which blocks are core vs adjacent vs emerging

evals.prompt: I have this validated discovery opportunity: 'New users don't know what to do after signup — 60% churn in the first week without ever completing a project.' Convert this into a proper bet with hypothesis, metrics, timebox, and kill criteria. Target outcome is improving 7-day activation rate from 40% to 60%.
evals.expected_output: A fully formatted bet with all required fields: hypothesis, target metric, timebox, block, kill criteria, and scale criteria
expectations: Output uses the bet format template with all required fields
Output writes a clear hypothesis statement (We believe that... will result in...)
Output specifies the target metric (40% → 60% 7-day activation)
Output includes clear kill criteria and scale criteria
Output links to the discovery opportunity
